compiled_state.title: dso:brainstorm: new page added without navigation path -> feature inaccessible via navigation
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

During a host project session, a user completed /dso:brainstorm for a new feature that created a new page. The feature was successfully implemented and the page was created, but no navigation link to the page was added.

## Expected Behavior

The brainstorm process should identify how users will reach the proposed feature. For features that create new pages or UI surfaces, brainstorm should explicitly ask: 'How will users navigate to this feature?' and surface this as a scope concern or story requirement. Not every new page needs global navigation (some are accessed via flow, modal, or deep link), but the brainstorm phase should explicitly capture the intended access path so the implementation plan includes the necessary navigation work.

## Actual Behavior

The brainstorm process completed without flagging that no navigation path was specified for the new page. The implementation plan did not include a navigation story. The page was built but was inaccessible via any navigation link.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run /dso:brainstorm for a feature that will produce a new page
2. Complete brainstorm without explicitly discussing navigation
3. Observe: no navigation gap is surfaced; implementation plan omits navigation link story

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Worktree
